summary: Project involves an admin page with message deletion and per-user pinned videos functionality on a backend system.
architectural_notes: Do not add any caller/principal checks to any backend function
Only make changes explicitly requested; do not modify unrelated pages or functions

existing_features.title: Re-enable message deletion on the admin page (frontend/src/pages/AdminDeleteChatroomsPage.tsx or a dedicated admin messages page). Expose a backend function to delete a message by ID with no caller check, and wire a delete button per message in the admin UI so admins can delete individual messages.

Change pinned video storage in the backend to be per-user rather than per-chatroom global. Each user's pinned video should be stored keyed by (chatroomId, userId) so that pinning or unpinning a video only affects the calling user's view. Other users in the same chatroom should not see or inherit another user's pinned video. Update the frontend to pass the current user's identity when fetching and mutating pinned videos, and render the pinned video only for the user who pinned it.

new_feature_requests.summary: Re-enable message deletion on the admin page
Remove all caller/principal checks from every backend function and do not add new ones
Make pinned videos per-user — each user only sees their own pinned videos, not other users'

Remove every caller/principal check from every function in backend/main.mo. This includes any use of 'caller', 'Principal.isAnonymous', ownership guards, or any other access control validation on any function. No caller checks should remain anywhere in the backend after this change.
